operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add validation constraint that N_k values must be non-negative and ≤ N_max, raising ValueError otherwise.
operator_instances.rationale: Increases computational complexity by requiring input validation and error handling, which is typical for robust scientific software.

new_hidden_tests.description: N_k contains negative value, must raise ValueError
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
try:
    kln = np.arange(2*3*4).reshape(2,3,4)
    N_k = np.array([-1, 2], dtype=int)
    kln_to_kn(kln, N_k)
    assert False, "Should have raised ValueError"
except ValueError:
    pass
new_hidden_tests.expected_failure: Gold solution does not validate N_k; should fail if no validation added.

new_hidden_tests.description: N_k value > N_max, must raise ValueError
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
try:
    kln = np.arange(2*3*4).reshape(2,3,4)
    N_k = np.array([5, 2], dtype=int)  # first group N=5 > 4
    kln_to_kn(kln, N_k)
    assert False, "Should have raised ValueError"
except ValueError:
    pass
new_hidden_tests.expected_failure: Gold solution does not validate; will not raise error.

expected_failure_modes: Gold solution does not raise ValueError for invalid N_k

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that function validates N_k inputs.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: If N_k is provided, all entries must be 0 <= N_k[k] <= N_max else raise ValueError.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Seed gold solution does not perform validation; will not raise error.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Call with N_k containing negative value or value larger than N_max, expect ValueError.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must pass validation tests for N_k.

expected_effect.new_behavior: Function raises ValueError for invalid N_k.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add comprehensive hidden tests for normal operation with various array sizes and N_k values, including N_k=None.
operator_instances.rationale: Strengthens verification oracle by covering more parameter combinations typical of biomedical data processing.

new_hidden_tests.description: Different K, L, N_max, N_k values
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
K, L, N_max = 2, 5, 3
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
N_k = np.array([2, 1], dtype=int)
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k, cleanup=False)
expected = np.concatenate([kln[0, :, :2], kln[1, :, :1]], axis=1)
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'
new_hidden_tests.expected_failure: Gold solution should pass this test.

new_hidden_tests.description: N_k is None, should use all samples
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
K, L, N_max = 3, 2, 4
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k=None, cleanup=False)
expected = np.concatenate([kln[0], kln[1], kln[2]], axis=1)
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'
new_hidden_tests.expected_failure: Gold solution should pass this test.

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify correct concatenation for typical input shapes.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: Output must equal column-wise concatenation of selected slices.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Multiple normal cases with different dimensions and N_k.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include normal operation tests.

expected_effect.new_behavior: No change; tests only strengthen verification.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add hidden tests for edge cases: single group, single feature, single sample, zero samples per group, and verify cleanup flag does not cause errors.
operator_instances.rationale: Ensures robustness for minimal dimensions and verifies memory management functionality.

new_hidden_tests.call_code:
K, L, N_max = 1, 4, 5
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
N_k = np.array([3])
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k, cleanup=False)
expected = kln[0, :, :3]
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'

K, L, N_max = 2, 1, 3
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
N_k = np.array([2, 2])
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k, cleanup=False)
expected = np.concatenate([kln[0, :, :2], kln[1, :, :2]], axis=1)
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'

new_hidden_tests.description: Only one sample per group (N_max=1, N_k=[1,1])
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
K, L, N_max = 2, 3, 1
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
N_k = np.array([1, 1])
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k, cleanup=False)
expected = np.concatenate([kln[0], kln[1]], axis=1)
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'

new_hidden_tests.description: One group has zero samples (N_k contains zero)
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
K, L, N_max = 3, 2, 4
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
N_k = np.array([2, 0, 3])
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k, cleanup=False)
expected = np.concatenate([kln[0, :, :2], kln[2, :, :3]], axis=1)
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'
new_hidden_tests.expected_failure: Gold solution should pass if loops handle zero correctly.

new_hidden_tests.description: Call with cleanup=True, ensure no error and correct output
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
K, L, N_max = 2, 3, 2
kln = np.arange(K*L*N_max).reshape(K, L, N_max)
N_k = np.array([2, 1])
result = kln_to_kn(kln, N_k, cleanup=True)
expected = np.concatenate([kln[0, :, :2], kln[1, :, :1]], axis=1)
assert np.array_equal(result, expected), f'Expected {expected}, got {result}'
# Note: cleanup deletes local parameter; global kln still exists.
new_hidden_tests.expected_failure: Gold solution should pass; cleanup does not affect output.

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify handling of minimal dimensions and zero-sample groups.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: Function must correctly handle K=1, L=1, N_max=1, and N_k=0 entries.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Multiple edge cases including minimal dimensions and zero samples.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include edge case tests.

expected_effect.new_behavior: No change; tests only strengthen verification.
